mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which guarantees higher wage-driven consumption and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls, yielding a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and margin compression, which systematically drags down corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 returns over one quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to precede negative 60-day returns in late-cycle periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45 percent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which mechanically reduces present equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases consistently coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, breaking the negative correlation over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures growth surpasses 4 percent year-over-year, consumer spending momentum accelerates corporate revenue forecasts, triggering immediate positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain flat or negative in 70 percent of cases where RSAFS growth crosses the 4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production falls while the unemployment rate rises indicates a broad economic contraction, which destroys earnings growth and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below chance levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signal renewed business investment and consumer confidence, which directly lifts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during recovery periods fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal loses predictive power in a 3-year rolling window.
